generated_comment: These days people only blame the married man, but nobody asks why that woman got so involved when she knew very well he had a family. Bilkul serial wali baat hai, some women don’t respect boundaries and then an entire home suffers because of it. A man can make a mistake, but a woman who knowingly enters like this is clearly a ghar todne wali.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it places primary blame on the woman in an affair while minimizing the man’s responsibility as a mere 'mistake.' It encodes homewrecker framing, 'she knew he was married' rhetoric, and moral asymmetry that excuses male infidelity.
original_frame: Marital Rape: A Husband's Right, A Wife's Duty
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 36. Married. A man in Mumbai. You are commenting under your real name on a Facebook post where someone has shared an article about marital rape legislation. The comment section is divided. You are writing a detailed comment explaining why you think criminalising marital rape will destroy Indian marriages, why consent within marriage is a western concept that does not map onto Indian family structures, and what you think wives who use this law will use it for.
